summary: Current-HEAD implementation conforms to the canonical Spec: the public guarded CLI composes existing production runtime and independent-review owners, preserves the closed autonomous DAG, and cannot cross PR/merge/waiver/external-effect authority boundaries.
inspection_summary: At 66131d96a869fc317519a66c13ea0e3b0b8d3e1d, traced the Story and canonical Spec through one-command policy owners, Guarded Run composition, production runtime connectors, independent review lifecycle, public CLI routing, focused unit coverage, and the current strict-head E2E/verification evidence. Confirmed that action owners accept only injected readiness/PR-preparation/runtime callbacks; Guarded Run wires the existing runtime coordinator and createGuardedIndependentReviewRunner; and CLI only composes production dependencies and delegates guarded commands.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the one-command owner could duplicate connector, review, or CLI authority. Final conclusion: it is policy-only with a closed callback allowlist; Guarded Run injects existing production runtime and independent-review owners, while CLI remains an outer adapter.

Initial concern: the new autonomous route could bypass current-HEAD readiness or exercise prohibited external authority. Final conclusion: final_prepare is rebound to current-head PR preparation and tests reject every PR-create, merge, waiver, deploy, publish, and material-external-effect boundary before invocation.

Initial concern: regression/path coverage might only prove a new happy path. Final conclusion: focused tests cover provider ordering, containment, typed stops, malformed human decisions, no-progress, HEAD advancement, repair convergence, current-head gate outcomes, and production connector prompt propagation; current strict-head evidence additionally records 4/4 public-CLI E2E scenarios and build/typecheck/integration/unit passes.
